members.name: Katy Horowitz
members.title: Head of School & Co-Founder
members.bio: Katy co-founded Pardes with a vision of building a school where every child is known by name and nurtured as an individual. With deep roots in Jewish education and community building, she leads with warmth, clarity, and an unwavering belief that children rise to the expectations set for them.
members.standout: Known for greeting every single student by name at morning drop-off — rain or shine.

members.name: Sharone Eichberg
members.title: Director & Co-Founder
members.bio: Sharone co-founded Pardes alongside Katy, bringing a passion for operations, family engagement, and building a school that truly serves its community. She ensures every detail — from enrollment to daily logistics — runs seamlessly so teachers can focus on teaching.
members.standout: The person families call when they need anything — and she always picks up.

members.name: Rabbi Dr. Levi Druin
members.title: Principal
members.bio: Rabbi Dr. Levi Druin leads the elementary division with a unique blend of Torah scholarship and hands-on leadership. He is in classrooms daily, knows every student personally, and believes that a great school is built one relationship at a time.
members.standout: You'll find him in the hallways more often than his office — that's by design.

members.name: Mimi Edwards
members.title: Principal, Middle School (MSAP)
members.bio: Mimi leads the Middle School Academy Program with a focus on preparing students for high school and beyond. She brings high expectations paired with genuine care, creating an environment where teenagers feel both challenged and supported.
members.standout: Believes every middle schooler deserves to be treated like the young adult they're becoming.

members.name: Adam Eshel
members.title: Dean of Students & LeHashlim Director
members.bio: Adam wears many hats — Dean of Students, director of the LeHashlim extended day program, and the person students seek out when they need someone in their corner. He builds relationships that help kids feel safe, seen, and ready to learn.
members.standout: Runs the afterschool program where homework actually gets done — and kids actually enjoy it.

members.name: Dina Kranz
members.title: Educational Consultant & Board Member
members.bio: Dina brings decades of educational expertise as a consultant and board member. She works behind the scenes to ensure Pardes maintains the highest academic standards while staying true to its mission of whole-child education.
members.standout: The strategic mind who helps Pardes think three steps ahead.

members.name: Chani Sharvit
members.title: Assistant Director, Preschool
members.bio: Chani helps lead the Gan Katan preschool program with a calm, nurturing presence. She supports teachers, coordinates curriculum, and ensures every little one feels at home from their very first day.
members.standout: Has a gift for turning a tearful first drop-off into a happy goodbye within minutes.

members.name: Fay Hertzel
members.title: Daisies, Lead Teacher
members.bio: Fay leads the Daisies classroom — our youngest learners — with patience, creativity, and a deep understanding of early childhood development. Her classroom is a world of discovery where toddlers build confidence through play.
members.standout: Her classroom is the one where toddlers run in happy on Monday mornings.

members.name: Lia Garcia
members.title: Daisies, Assistant
members.bio: Lia brings warmth and attentiveness to the Daisies classroom, ensuring every toddler feels secure and cared for throughout the day. She's the steady, reassuring presence that helps our youngest students thrive.
members.standout: The gentle hands that make every child feel like the most important person in the room.

members.name: Lilly Tyagney
members.title: Daisies, Assistant
members.bio: Lilly supports the Daisies classroom with energy and heart. She helps create a joyful, safe space where toddlers explore, play, and begin their journey at Pardes.
members.standout: Always the first to get down on the floor and play at a child's level.

members.name: Michal Hen
members.title: Sunflowers, Lead Teacher
members.bio: Michal leads the Sunflowers classroom with a focus on curiosity, language development, and building early social skills. Her Israeli background brings Hebrew naturally into daily classroom life.
members.standout: Her students sing Hebrew songs at home that their parents can't help but learn too.

members.name: Marlene Villota
members.title: Sunflowers, Assistant
members.bio: Marlene brings patience and care to the Sunflowers classroom, supporting children as they grow in independence and confidence. She's a consistent, loving presence for families.
members.standout: Parents say she remembers every detail about every child — because she does.

members.name: Pnina Amir
members.title: Sunflowers, Assistant
members.bio: Pnina brings bilingual warmth to the Sunflowers classroom, helping children feel comfortable in both English and Hebrew. Her gentle approach makes transitions throughout the day feel easy.
members.standout: Bridges languages and cultures so naturally, children don't even notice the switch.

members.name: Tali Epelbaum
members.title: Sunflowers, Assistant
members.bio: Tali supports the Sunflowers classroom with creativity and a nurturing spirit. She helps design activities that spark curiosity and keep little learners engaged all day.
members.standout: Has a talent for turning everyday moments into learning opportunities.

members.name: Sarah Wachtel
members.title: Caterpillars, Lead Teacher
members.bio: Sarah leads the Caterpillars classroom — our pre-K three-year-olds — with a warm, structured approach that builds school readiness through meaningful play and hands-on learning.
members.standout: Her classroom is where kids first discover that learning and fun are the same thing.

members.name: Sarah Hecht
members.title: Caterpillars, Assistant
members.bio: Sarah brings attentiveness and joy to the Caterpillars classroom. She works closely with each child, supporting their growth and celebrating every milestone along the way.
members.standout: Celebrates every small victory — first letters written, shoes tied, names spelled — like it's a big deal. Because it is.

members.name: Yennifer Guzman
members.title: Caterpillars, Assistant
members.bio: Yennifer supports the Caterpillars classroom with dedication and a caring heart. She helps ensure every child feels comfortable, happy, and ready to explore.
members.standout: Her calm energy makes even the busiest moments feel manageable.

members.name: Lieba Rosenberg-Costello
members.title: Butterflies, Lead Teacher
members.bio: Lieba leads the Butterflies — our Pre-K classroom — preparing children for their transition to kindergarten with a thoughtful blend of academic readiness, creative expression, and Judaic learning.
members.standout: By graduation, her students are reading, writing, and ready — and they don't want to leave.

members.name: Betty Torres
members.title: Butterflies, Assistant
members.bio: Betty brings warmth and dedication to the Butterflies classroom. She supports children through their important Pre-K year with patience and encouragement.
members.standout: The calm, steady presence that makes the Pre-K year feel safe and special.

members.name: Mary Sanchez
members.title: Butterflies, Assistant
members.bio: Mary helps create a nurturing environment in the Butterflies classroom where every child feels valued and supported as they prepare for their next big step.
members.standout: Children gravitate to her because they know she's always paying attention.

members.name: Menucha Birenbaum
members.title: Butterflies, Assistant
members.bio: Menucha brings a warm Judaic presence to the Butterflies classroom, helping children connect to Jewish traditions through stories, songs, and daily rituals.
members.standout: Makes Shabbat prep the highlight of every Friday.

members.name: Yossi Brock
members.title: Principal Assistant
members.bio: Yossi supports the elementary school leadership team and helps keep daily operations running smoothly. He's the behind-the-scenes problem-solver who makes sure nothing falls through the cracks.
members.standout: The first person to arrive and the last to leave — and always with a smile.

members.name: Sabrina Sole
members.title: Office Admin
members.bio: Sabrina is the welcoming face of the elementary office. She manages communications, logistics, and the countless daily requests that keep a school running.
members.standout: If you need something done, Sabrina already has it handled.

members.name: Camryn Winograd
members.title: Kindergarten, General Studies
members.bio: Camryn brings creativity and structure to the kindergarten general studies classroom. She builds a love of reading and math from day one, meeting each child exactly where they are.
members.standout: Her reading corner is where kindergartners discover that books are magic.

members.name: Sima Masarano
members.title: Kindergarten, Judaic Studies
members.bio: Sima teaches kindergarten Judaic studies with warmth and authenticity. She brings Jewish holidays, Hebrew, and Torah stories to life in ways that five-year-olds find genuinely exciting.
members.standout: Her Shabbat preparations are so beloved that parents have started adopting her traditions at home.

members.name: Chaya Gerber-Katz
members.title: Kindergarten, Assistant
members.bio: Chaya supports the kindergarten classroom with patience and care. She works alongside lead teachers to ensure every child gets the individual attention they need during this foundational year.
members.standout: Has an instinct for knowing which child needs a quiet word of encouragement — and when.

members.name: Jenny Emsalem
members.title: 1st Grade, General Studies
members.bio: Jenny teaches first grade general studies with enthusiasm and high expectations. She builds strong readers, confident writers, and curious thinkers — all while making learning feel like an adventure.
members.standout: Her students write stories they're actually proud to read out loud.

members.name: Stephanie Nigri
members.title: 1st Grade, Judaic Studies
members.bio: Stephanie teaches first grade Judaic studies, connecting children to Hebrew language, Jewish values, and Torah in a way that feels personal and meaningful.
members.standout: Finds a way to relate every Torah lesson to something in her students' real lives.

members.name: Noa Castro
members.title: 1st Grade, Assistant
members.bio: Noa supports the first grade team with energy and attentiveness. She ensures each child feels supported as they navigate the exciting challenges of first grade.
members.standout: The extra pair of eyes that catches every hand raised, every question unasked.

members.name: Chaya Sara Barrocas
members.title: 2nd Grade, General Studies
members.bio: Chaya Sara brings structure and warmth to the second grade general studies classroom. She builds foundational academic skills while nurturing each student's unique strengths.
members.standout: Her classroom runs like clockwork, but feels like home.

members.name: Mushky Druin
members.title: 2nd Grade, Judaic Studies
members.bio: Mushky teaches second grade Judaic studies with creativity and heart. She makes Hebrew and Torah learning engaging and accessible, building a strong Jewish identity in every student.
members.standout: Her students look forward to Chumash class — and that says everything.

members.name: Chaya Mushka Fuchs
members.title: 2nd Grade, Assistant
members.bio: Chaya Mushka supports the second grade classroom with dedication and a warm presence. She provides individualized attention that helps every student feel confident and capable.
members.standout: Quietly makes sure no student ever feels behind or left out.

members.name: Henya Gutnick
members.title: 3rd Grade, Judaic Studies
members.bio: Henya teaches third grade Judaic studies, deepening students' connection to Hebrew, Torah, and Jewish life. Her classroom is a place of joyful, rigorous learning.
members.standout: Her students' Hebrew reading skills surprise even their parents.

members.name: Zahava Stilman
members.title: 3rd Grade, General Studies
members.bio: Zahava teaches third grade general studies with a focus on critical thinking and a love of learning. She challenges students while making sure everyone feels capable of rising to the occasion.
members.standout: Known for the kinds of math problems that make students argue — in the best way.

members.name: Shoshana Gertz
members.title: 3rd Grade, Assistant
members.bio: Shoshana supports the third grade team, providing the extra attention and care that helps each student reach their potential during this pivotal year.
members.standout: The go-to person when a student needs someone to listen.

members.name: Felicia John
members.title: 4th Grade, General Studies
members.bio: Felicia teaches fourth grade general studies, preparing students for the academic demands of upper elementary with engaging, hands-on instruction that keeps students invested in their learning.
members.standout: Her science experiments are the ones students talk about at dinner.

members.name: Yehudis Herrman
members.title: 4th Grade, Judaic Studies
members.bio: Yehudis teaches fourth grade Judaic studies with depth and passion. She guides students into more complex Torah study while keeping the joy and relevance front and center.
members.standout: Asks the kinds of questions that make fourth graders think — really think.

members.name: Aniaha Wesley
members.title: 5th Grade, General Studies
members.bio: Aniaha teaches fifth grade general studies, bringing energy and high expectations to the upper elementary classroom. She prepares students for middle school while celebrating who they are right now.
members.standout: Her students leave fifth grade feeling ready for anything.

members.name: Maytar Jerufi
members.title: 5th Grade, Judaic Studies
members.bio: Maytar teaches fifth grade Judaic studies with a dynamic, interactive approach. She brings Hebrew and Torah alive for students who are ready for deeper learning and critical engagement.
members.standout: Makes ancient texts feel surprisingly relevant to a ten-year-old's life.

members.name: Moshe Barrocas
members.title: 5th Grade, Judaic Studies
members.bio: Moshe teaches fifth grade Judaic studies with a focus on building strong Torah skills and Jewish identity. His calm, thoughtful teaching style earns the trust and respect of his students.
members.standout: The kind of teacher students remember years later.

members.name: Desbe Hafif
members.title: Office Admin, Middle School
members.bio: Desbe manages the Middle School office with efficiency and warmth. She's the organizational backbone of the MSAP campus, keeping everything running so students and teachers can focus on learning.
members.standout: If you need to know anything about the middle school schedule, Desbe knows.

members.name: Jared Ruffin
members.title: General Studies, Middle School
members.bio: Jared teaches general studies in the middle school, bringing engaging instruction and real-world connections to his classes. He pushes students to think critically while building their confidence.
members.standout: Makes middle school academics feel like preparation for real life, not just tests.

members.name: Nava Taieb
members.title: General Studies, Middle School
members.bio: Nava teaches general studies in the middle school with passion and purpose. She creates a classroom environment where students feel comfortable taking intellectual risks.
members.standout: Her students aren't afraid to be wrong — because she's taught them that's how you learn.

members.name: Yishai Klein
members.title: Judaic Studies, Middle School
members.bio: Yishai teaches Judaic studies in the middle school, guiding students through sophisticated Torah learning and helping them build a mature, meaningful Jewish identity.
members.standout: His Talmud classes spark debates that continue long after the bell rings.

members.name: Malka Meyers
members.title: Judaic Studies, Middle School
members.bio: Malka teaches Judaic studies in the middle school with depth and compassion. She meets students where they are and helps them find personal meaning in Jewish learning.
members.standout: Creates a space where every question is welcome — especially the tough ones.

members.name: Ovadya Rogalsky
members.title: Judaic Studies, Middle School
members.bio: Ovadya teaches Judaic studies in the middle school, bringing energy and authenticity to Torah and Hebrew instruction. He connects with students through genuine relationships and meaningful learning.
members.standout: His enthusiasm is contagious — students actually look forward to learning Gemara.

members.name: Geoff Campbell
members.title: P.E. Teacher (2nd-5th)
members.bio: Geoff leads physical education for the elementary grades, building teamwork, sportsmanship, and a love of movement. His classes are the highlight of many students' days.
members.standout: Turns PE into the class where every kid — athletic or not — feels like a winner.

members.name: Coach Ariel
members.title: P.E. Teacher (Daisies-1st)
members.bio: Coach Ariel teaches physical education for the youngest students at Pardes, from Daisies through first grade. He makes movement fun, builds coordination, and helps little ones develop confidence in their bodies.
members.standout: Even the shyest toddlers come alive during Coach Ariel's class.
